sources.label: RWC ASX announcements page (latest lodgement pre-dates June 6)
Capital Brief - RWC maintains FY26 guidance after US tariff refund claim (April 2026, latest material update)
ASX announcement 28 April 2026 - RWC confirms FY26 full year trading outlook

verdicts.rankRationale: No company-specific news since June 6; today's +3.5% uptick is a flow-driven bounce in a name still down ~15% YTD, which neither adds evidence for the water-loop/rapid-build pathway nor changes its latent-node optionality. Yesterday's hold stands.
verdicts.bull: Pipe fittings, valves and flow-control products are exactly the unglamorous plumbing layer needed for data-center liquid-cooling loops and fast facility builds, and at -15% YTD with a $1.7B cap the name carries zero AI premium - pure free optionality if hyperscale water-loop demand reaches it.
verdicts.neutral: RWC remains a residential repair-and-renovation plumbing business; FY26 guidance was merely reaffirmed in April with HY26 EBITDA down 22.5%, and there is still no disclosed data-center or AI-infrastructure revenue to validate the pathway.
verdicts.bear: The AI water-loop linkage is speculative while the core business faces soft US/UK housing demand and a residual US$25M+ tariff drag; if cooling-loop spend flows to specialist industrial valve and CDU makers instead, RWC never participates and stays a cyclical housing name.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Any disclosed data-center, liquid-cooling or commercial mechanical-contractor channel win, or FY26 results (August) showing tariff impact at the low end with H2 sales recovery.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Continued US/UK residential demand weakness plus tariff costs compress earnings further, with no AI-adjacent revenue ever materializing to re-rate the latent-node thesis.
